Arrington de Dionyso.title: Abraxasaxophonic Smooth Jazz Vagina
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
VIDEO HERE:  www.youtube.com/watch?v=qH-hOug9fFU
Arrington de Dionyso (Old Time Relijun and Malaikat Dan Singa) offers a solid 20 minutes of abrasive and bizarre free form saxophone work over the distorted reception of a smooth jazz radio station. Confusing yet strangely honest feeling. The first half of the flip side features several short pieces recorded in the same era (some sounding like fragments of Old Time Relijun pieces, some more in-line with Arrington's solo work), with the second half pulled from his masterpiece, "Slow Dancin' Under the Moon Tonight", about which he said: "...was recorded sometime in 2010 using "Songsmith" software. It is one of the most unlistenable and abrasively grating albums ever made by Arrington de Dionyso". Enjoy!
Arrington de Dionyso.credits:
released January 1, 2001
From the original liner notes 2-25-2000
ABRAXASAXOPHONIC SMOOTH JAZZ VAGINA
automatic gematria of the dada-kabbalah revelation,
a blight on the hothouse clones
ancient awkward bleating dithyrambs unfurled,
a thunderous molten mass of honey
undug from the pharoah's sepulchre-
putid, mite-infested, but laced with gold-
a spark of light, sneaky dreaming in the rotten
core of the rabid radio plastic.
To the thunder of the perfect tidal mind,
a broken be-bop mirror, intensity enslaved
in density of clumsy lead-
the primordial antiphony of natural protest,
a sparkling jewel lodged deep in the gargling
throat of the behemoth, lapis lazuli leaking
from the cracks of the crocodile's jawbone unhinged,
slipping come-soaked fingers
through the underbrush of sacred cunt.
A mutiny of clashing forms,
a relijun of gigantomachia in the triple circus,
from coloseum to catacomb, a crawling of culture into the sea.
We are the post-shamans of the blackest rage.
The ripped-apart, unrendered god is ONE in MANY
scattered, shattered, torn-apart pieces.
This work is the beginning of a twenty-two volume set,
an extraction of the fermented chunks of god-flesh
buried deep within the commodity culture.
This is a work of intimacy and archaeology, fixing glue
to the scattered shards, mending the hole
in the mirror of the world dream.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: ALKISAH (Senyawa dan Singo REMIX) Benefit For Palu, Indonesia
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Senyawa remixed by Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Indonesian band SENYAWA has made history with the release of "Alkisah" which will definitively be remembered among the best albums of 2021. By allowing the album to be released and remixed by almost 50 different labels they are completely undermining and revolutionizing the way that music is shared in the trans-modern landscape.
I have been an active collaborator with Rully and Wukir since 2011- working together with them on our earliest multi-city tour of Indonesia as part of the Malaikat dan Singa "Suara Bahaya Tour" organized by Yes No Wave Music. We have performed and recorded together on many other occasions including my bandcamp release "Unheard Indonesia Vol. 5" and at our residency at Pioneer Works in NYC in 2016 after I organized the band's first ever concerts in the United States.
I am overjoyed to bring you my own personal "remix" of ALKISAH made with the band's blessing. It is temporarily set to "pay what you want" download so that our friends in Indonesia will be able to enjoy, but please donate $10-20 if you can afford it, this is a fundraiser for our community of artists and musicians in Palu, Sulawesi to help them preserve endangered traditions and bring vibrancy to a community devastated by recent earthquakes, tsunamis and mudslides. A "physical" CDR version is also available.
See also the parallel release today of "Elo Progo Improvisations" which was recorded with Rully in 2013. These improvisations were recorded in the same location as where the band built their studio to make "Alkisah"- at the Tempuran Elo Progo near Borobodur. It is the meeting point of the mighty Elo and Progo Rivers in Central Java and a very potent sacred location. It is said that in early Buddhist history Lama Atisha travelled from Bengal to this exact area of Central Java where he meditated for twelve years before returning to the continent to become a founder of Yellow Hat Buddhism in Tibet.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Big Ears on the Lookout- Benefit for RAICES Texas
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: This Saxophone Kills Fascists with Greg Saunier
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
note: ALL funds raised by donations (minus the percentage deducted by bandcamp) are towards the benefit of RAICES Texas  www.raicestexas.org
END CONCENTRATION CAMPS IN THE US AND EVERYWHERE ELSE
THIS SAXOPHONE KILLS FASCISTS
Arrington de Dionyso uses the transcendent power of tenor and baritone saxophones and the bass clarinet to surround hatred with the harmonic vibrations necessary to neutralize it and spread a message of liberation for all oppressed people.
These recordings document an ongoing collaboration between This Saxophone Kills Fascists and DEERHOOF drummer Greg Saunier. The first track on the album is a live concert at BIG EARS MUSIC FESTIVAL in Knoxville, Tennessee in May 2017. The concert features Steve Gigante (of Old Man Fuck You, Dark Inside the Sun, W-S Burn, Fork in the Blender Music) on bass.
Track two and three were recorded during an artist residency at the Lookout Arts Quarry in Skagit County, Washington.
Arrington de Dionyso.credits:
released July 4, 2019
Arrington de Dionyso- tenor saxophone, baritone saxophone, bass clarinet
Greg Saunier- drums
Steve Gigante- bass on track 1

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Bobcat Flamethroat (1993)
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
"Bobcatflamethroat" was originally released as "Pine Cone Alley Cassette #9" in August of 1993. The songs were recorded on a Tascam Porta-One 4 Track cassette studio inside a secret area in the basement of the College Activities Building at the Evergreen State College, known as "Happyland". This album has never before seen a digital release of any kind, however there is one song "Everything is Broken" which later became part of the original "canon" of Old Time Relijun after that band was formed in 1995. That song was re-recorded on the first Old Time Relijun album "Songbook Vol. I" released in 1997.
I still dig most of the tunes on this one- these were all written and recorded while preparing to welcome a new young life into the world (my daughter Lucinda, born August 22, 1993). So while not specifically "Children's Music" per se, the tunes are wild, hopeful, optimistic yawps of playful abandon for all ages. There are a number of "inside jokes" that only would have made sense to the very tight knit inner circle hat I considered my "core" group of friends at that point in my life. I also think there are more than a few "hits" on here. I was 18 years old!
Anyone who has followed the last thirty years of my musical career should find something of interest and delight on this album. For some reason I chose to record most of the guitar and bass parts "direct" without an amplifier- I'm not sure why I did that but it's a unique sound in retrospect. There's a decent dose of throatsinging and other odd vocal techniques, proving that I dove deep into this territory of vocal exploration at a very young age. Also plenty of mouth harps, flutes, kazoos, and clarinet, although this was just BEFORE I bought my first bass clarinet. The song "Kite Dragon Hypnosis" showcases the very first time I EVER recorded anything with a saxophone!
The lyrics are reflective of my interests in the theories of "Ethnopoetics" as put forth by Jerome Rothenberg in many of his books such as "Shaking the Pumpkin" and "Technicians of the Sacred", as pathways to understanding the universality of myth and shamanism as connective threads through human poetic expression. And yes, if you know something about the Evergreen State College, I did indeed receive 16 credits for working on this album.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Breath of Fire (KLP176)
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Arrington de Dionyso is an energy conduit, driving through the territories held between surrealist automatism, shamanic séance, and the folk imagery of rock and roll. Breath of Fire is Arrington's first true solo album, and its sounds are hysterical, intellectual, magical, hilarious, sad, ecstatic and beautiful.
While Arrington's work with Old Time Relijun has brought much in the way of both critical praise and notoriety for ecstatic-rock deconstructions and a frenzied performance on the edge of possession, Breath of Fire is a very different sort of beast. Recorded on Christmas Day in a nameless town in Southern Italy, the album uses voice, bass clarinet, and jaw harps to invoke the spirits in a mind-bending ritual of technical mastery and emotional freedom. Like field recordings from a lost continent, these short pieces conspire to present a completely mysterious sound universe, unlike anything you may have heard before. To be sure this voyage is not without its reference points- fans of Old Time Relijun's live performances have longtime been aware of Arrington's multiphonic vocal research, unearthing the missing links between Tuvan throat-singing and free improvisation. In Breath of Fire, this research is presented in its purest form- spontaneously, without overdubs or processing of any kind; an ethnography of intergalactic avant-primitive folk music, a dream diary without words, that enwraps in resonant sound.
Old Time Relijun has released seven albums since 1995, building an ever-fanatic cult following in the USA, Canada, and Europe. Arrington de Dionyso tours constantly throughout the USA, Europe, and Israel giving performances and workshops on the voice in free improvised music

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Elo Progo Improvisations
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso and Rully Shabara
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
In celebration of the release of Senyawa's new album ALKISAH through a mycelial network of nearly 50 different labels and remixers worldwide, we also offer "Elo Progo Improvisations" recorded by Arrington de Dionyso and Rully Shabara (vocalist of Senyawa) in 2013. These recordings were made near the same site where Alkisah was made- but outside under the trees, not inside a studio- at Tempuran Elo Progo- where the Elo and the Progo River meet in Central Java, very close to the ancient site of Borobodur. It is said that Lama Atisha travelled from Bengal to Elo Progo where he meditated for many years before returning to the continent to become a founder of Yellow Hat Buddhism in Tibet.
In these improvisations for voices, bromiophone, rubber bands and mirlitons, we tap into the sounds of wind and flowing water and harness those powers as an energetic strength. Some video was also recorded of this riverside session!
www.youtube.com/watch?v=cXY-CA08xPI&t=130s
As an act of Sacred Reciprocity, this album is offered as a "pay any amount" release so that our friends in Indonesia may enjoy our work together without having to pay for the download. If you live in the United States, Canada, or Europe, etc and have the benefit of a PayPal account with funds, you are requested to offer an amount you feel comfortable with but at least $10 would be ideal. There is also a "physical" CDR release available as a double album that includes my "Senyawa dan Singo" remix of ALKISAH.
Half of the funds raised from this album will be directed to the benefit of our community of artists and musicians in Palu, Sulawesi, to assist in the preservation of traditional music and the sustaining of life in a community affected greatly by recent natural disasters (earthquake, tsunami, mudslides) that happened even before the Covid-19 pandemic even began.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Gamelan Suara Baru and Arrington de Dionyso: Live at PSKaufman Gallery
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso and Gamelan Suara Baru
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
ALL SALES/DONATIONS RAISED WILL BENEFIT ART AND MUSIC COMMUNITIES RECOVERING FROM THE EARTHQUAKE AND TSUNAMI IN PALU, INDONESIA
We are very pleased to be able to share this live recording made at PSKaufman Gallery in Downtown Los Angeles on January 20th, 2019. This recording documents a unique collaboration between musician and painter Arrington de Dionyso (playing bass clarinet and saxophone on this session) and the members of Gamelan Suara Baru.
Gamelan Suara Baru is an experimental Javanese gamelan collective based out of California Institute of the Arts. The brainchild of composer/performer, Sean Hayward, the group is known for its far-reaching collaborations. Recent projects include a full length album of original compositions for gamelan and metal music (to be released in April 2019), and an evening length performance of Lou Harrison’s gamelan works at REDCAT.
In the spirit of forging closer ties between students and lovers of Indonesian music in the US and communities of artists in need living in Indonesia, we offer the music contained herein in a spirit of peace and friendship to our peers in Palu, Sulawesi who are rebuilding their lives after suffering from a devastating earthquake and tsunami on September 30 2018. Any and all funds raised by downloading this recording will be directed to one of several different musicians in a position of community leadership in Palu.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Honey and Poison (2019) MALAIKAT DAN SINGA
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
This is the long-awaited follow up to 2013's "Arrington de Dionyso's Malaikat dan Singa: OPEN THE CROWN".
The online release of this album is a bittersweet admission of defeat and a triumphant if temporary return to songcraft. Recording began at Dub Narcotic Studio towards the end of 2013 and continued in spurts throughout 2014. Final mixes were made in 2017 but the recordings were not mastered until late in 2018. In many ways I have thought of "Honey and Poison" as both my "Lost Album" and perhaps my "Last Album" as I have seen the sad decay and transformation of my longstanding record label and the closing and sale of the recording studio that I had worked with for over twenty years. I had plans to press a vinyl version of this album but in the months leading up the 2016 presidential election my hopes for that happening greatly diminished. There is no room for this music in the "music industry" and no room for this music in the era of Trump and Fascism. This is trance world Trans-Utopian music, it is made for a world of imagination, a celebration of Life and a conversation with Death. I don't think I have ever recorded an album of "songs" so immediately meaningful and personally "important" to me. No record label is interested in releasing this, no publicist is interested in promoting this. It is up to YOU, the person reading this, to listen, to share, to say whatever it is you might like to say about the music contained in this virtual package "Honey and Poison". Thank you for listening.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: I See Beyond The Black Sun (KLP200)
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Light passes through a prism and fractures to create a rainbow effect; the color spectrum breaks off from every angle. It appears as if the colors divide themselves, but in reality they bleed in together – overlapping and mingling. It is within this closely inspected area of bleeding and mingling that Arrington de Dionyso creates his latest solo work I See Beyond the Black Sun [KLP200].
De Dionyso explores tones in terms of specific individual sounds, much like each minute shift of color across the spectrum. Interchanging bass clarinet and throat-singing with homemade instruments used as vocal modifiers, I See Beyond the Black Sun is less the apocalyptic doom of his ramshackle post-punk group Old Time Relijun, but instead a raga influenced anthropological meditation. Locating the tones between tones and the overtones within overtones – seeing beyond the black sun.
I See Beyond the Black Sun pushes de Dionyso's previous solo album Breath of Fire [KLP176] (2006) from a charcoal sketchbook into a Technicolor landscape with more clarity, shape and definition. The album is structured with two immense bookends "AION" and "Pluto in Capricorn (I See Beyond the Black Sun)." "AION" creates arhythmic drone that specifies the rules for movements of the melodies on the album in a convergent up and down scale – continuing to climb the color spectrum ever so slowly. The gradient shift is deceptively hidden before exploding into color on the parenthetical title track. "Pluto in Capricorn (I See Beyond the Black Sun)" – featuring a guest appearance by Old Time Relijun drummer Germaine Baca – is the culminating cathartic conclusion, a propulsive mythical beast that combines all the previous pieces into a new separate entity where sparks begin to rise. It is the crossing over and the release.
"This music winds like an infinite snake around the vine of a central note, probing hypnotically as Mr. de Dionyso … inexhaustibly exhales mandalas, spirals, unknowable cuneiforms and other beguiling shapes, while simultaneously driving the pulse and drone to advance, like a slow motion tsunami, towards a shocking climax of release … Crucial listening for those who want music and sound that reaches back into the center of your head." - Michael Gira, Angels of Light/Swans

This concert was recorded at The Chapel Performance Space at the Good Shepherd Center in Seattle on February 22, 2019.
The recording showcases the unique duo language developed between bass clarinetist (also playing Lalove, the sacred flute of Central Sulawesi) Arrington de Dionyso, with Ahmad Yousefbeigi, a master of traditional Kurdish percussion instruments such as the Daf, and also a seasoned improvisor. For the last third of the concert we are joined by Amy Denio on vocals and clarinet.
Ahmad ends the show with a solo performance of a traditional Kurdish song, dedicated to the memory of his brother.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Low Clarinet Duos 2009
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Paul Hoskin and Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Paul Hoskin you will be missed my friend.
Rest in Peace
1959-2018
Everyone close to him was aware that his health was in decline, so I wasn't really "surprised" to learn we had lost Paul...but as the days have passed by the impact of that loss has dug into me deeper.
When I first met Paul Hoskin in 1995, I was only twenty, and he was the first person I had ever met who shared such enthusiasm for the powerful sounds of the bass and contrabass clarinet family. I had only been playing for a few years, so meeting him through the Olympia Experimental Music Festival and later at the Seattle Improvised Music Festival was a profound encounter for me. Paul had dedicated his musical life to the fervent explorations of the uncharted outer limits of free improvisation through his instruments, for which he had developed uniquely unparalleled voicings and multiphonic techniques through which he tapped into a raw, unbridled expression bringing the listener directly in physical touch with PRIMORDIAL SOUND.
In his absence I would like to dedicate to his memory and to his many friends and musical co-collaborators the publishing of these duo sessions from 2009. Paul performs exclusively on contrabass clarinet, while I am on bass clarinet and contra-alto clarinet. Tracks 7 and 8 features some vocals through the body of the instrument, which in certain moments is modified by aluminum foil and a snare drum placed next to the bell.
The final track appears to have been recorded at an art gallery in Astoria, Oregon, I'm uncertain of the exact date in 2008 or 2009.
UPDATED: Obituary written by Steve Peters
Paul Hoskin 1958-2018
By Steve Peters
Paul Hoskin, a key figure in Seattle’s history of freely improvised music, passed away in his Seattle apartment on November 17.
While attending Oberlin College in the late ‘70s, Hoskin hung out with musicians and listened voraciously, especially to free jazz and European improvisation. In 1980 his brother gave him a battered C-melody saxophone salvaged from a friend’s attic and admonished him to start playing. Essentially self-taught, Hoskin worked his way through the reed family, eventually settling on contrabass clarinet and baritone sax, on which he developed a highly idiosyncratic voice that never ceased to evolve.
In the ‘80s and ‘90s Hoskin bounced between the coasts and toured around the US and Europe. He improvised relentlessly, playing in countless ad hoc groupings, as well as ongoing projects like Audio Letter and the sprawling New Art Orchestra in Seattle, and NYC groups The Same and Trigger. He also collaborated extensively with dancers, especially Linda Austin and Margit Galanter. He returned to the Northwest in the late ‘90s, moving around from the Olympic Peninsula to Astoria, Oregon, until finally settling down in Seattle in 2009.
Hoskin was a tireless catalyst in the improvised music community, always seeking out new players and venues and curating shows. In 1985 he organized a weekend gathering of locals and friends from New York to create the first Seattle Improvised Music Festival, now entering its 34th year. He continued to be involved with it sporadically, returning as co-curator in 2015 and 2016. He also booked shows at the Collins Pub, and was involved with Gallery 1412 and Spite House.
Since 2016, Hoskin stepped back from music, attending the occasional gig but mostly laying low. No stranger to hard luck and hard living, his health deteriorated in the last few years, resulting in several brushes with mortality followed by astonishing recoveries, of which he was quite proud; he relished defying everyone’s worst expectations. But in recent months he resumed practicing, and spoke optimistically of returning to performing next year. He also privately expressed profound gratitude to the community that had helped nurture and support him through his various crises.
Paul Hoskin touched a lot of lives and was beloved by many, even though loving him could be a challenge. He was full of contradictions: endearing, exasperating, brilliant, feral, loyal, stubborn, dedicated, self-deprecating, grandiose, gentle, dismissive, meticulous, chaotic, self-destructive, resilient. He might expound eloquently on Spinoza, Heidegger, and Wittgenstein, or recall a long, factually questionable story from 30 years ago. He lived on his own terms, made a deep impression on the music scene in Seattle and elsewhere, and leaves an equally large void in his absence. Quite simply, there was no one like him.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Malaikat dan Singa (KLP215)
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Malaikat Dan Singa [KLP215] is a collection of throbbing, hallucinogenic, trance-inducing trans-utopian cosmic post-punk sounds from Old Time Relijun’s Arrington de Dionyso. It was recorded at the Dub Narcotic Studio in Olympia, Washington, by Karl Blau. Never one to compromise in pursuit of his twisted visions of the future of music, de Dionyso not only rattles listeners with heavy grooves but he also sings every song on this album in Indonesian. What? Arrington tells us that some of the lyrics are lifted from the poems of William Blake and passages from The Zohar.
Musically speaking, despite the Indonesian influence, de Dionyso doesn’t survey continents in search of exotic sounds. Instead he drills holes through them with a contemporary urgency. Malaikat Dan Singa is an album of translated and personal mysticism that sounds like it was dug up from the Earth. Left here from a future time, unknown to us in the present.
Vast tracts of the uncharitable cavernous underground is exposed in this imagined musical Pangaea by using microtones, polyrhythms, throat singing, echoplex, bass clarinet and reverb in addition to drums, guitar and bass (three of the songs on this album include the Old Time Relijun line-up). Although there is a familiar and foreign rhythm with its mixture of post-punk and Gamelan tendencies, Malaikat Dan Singa is really Future World Music. Maybe it will be used to sell transportive vehicles in 2369. Or a DNA replacement cream. And you can dance to it.
Some artists push the envelope, de Dionyso eats the envelope and spits out an origami of sound. And he says he recorded this album to impress a girl. It was followed up by another excursion into Indonesian dance raga by Arrington de Dionyso's Malaikat dan Singa, Suara Naga [KLP226].
“Arrington de Dionyso’s … ravishingly alien sound [is] deep, guttural, trance-inducing and overpoweringly physical.” - Plan B Magazine
"This is a great record--who else is going to be crazy enough to do an entire American release sung in Indonesian? It's as if Arrington stole Tom Waits' old band, dragged them to 1974 Bandung, slipped them some mescaline, made them listen to BROERY at maximum volume until they begged him to stop the cassette and then took them to record the whole thing in a cloud of burning smoke on the lip of Krakatoa." - Alan Bishop, Sublime Frequencies/Sun City Girls

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Medicine Blues Tapes
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
At this crucially strange juncture of human history, Arrington de Dionyso releases what is perhaps his most radical statement yet in MEDICINE BLUES TAPES, nearly two hours of Medicine Songs, Algysh chants, Shamanic Blues, Shouts and Hollers. Ethnopoetically inspired and spiritually FIRED UP to a Shaking Fever- these are songs for confronting all demons, corruption and oppression, for extracting all poisons and restoring right relations between Planet Earth and the ever outward expanding Omniverse. Using CASSETTE TAPE as a medium for transmitting a magnetized message, I hope these songs set you on fire, shake you to your core, and change your life forever. DEATH TO ALL DICTATORS.
NOTE: The "merch item" that accompanies the Medicine Blues Tapes digital download is a completely UNIQUE session of all-analog Medicine Songs recorded directly to magnetic tape and "made to order". This is an experiment in developing new strategies of resistance, artistic integrity and survival for a living musician in this fucking insane age where every single resource and collective wealth is being robbed from us blindly seemingly without recourse.
These songs are MEDICINE and these tapes will be tailored to your unique needs as an act of Psychomagic. You do not need to listen to the entire tape at once, but it will aid you in whatever spiritual work you do for personal or collective healing, and for the energetic protection of yourself and those you care about most deeply. AUTHENTIC Intelligence versus "Artificial Intelligence"!
You will receive THE ACTUAL recording itself, not a copy. I will not keep a personal copy for myself, if you order this item you will have the sole unique copy of 90 minutes of Medicine Songs made for you- how you choose to share with others is up to you.
The cover packaging will also include a small hand-made painting created uniquely for you. Please allow at least two weeks for delivery.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Minneapolis to Malang
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso with International Novelty Gamelan
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
The first section of this album documents a live collaboration between saxophonist Arrington de Dionyso and International Novelty Gamelan that took place on 4/21/17 in Minneapolis during Arrington's THIS SAXOPHONE KILLS FASCISTS national US tour.
Arrington de Dionyso plays baritone and tenor sax, bass clarinet, and bromiophones.
China Faith Star joins on soprano saxophone for a portion of the show.
INTERNATIONAL NOVELTY GAMELAN IS:
Kevin Cosgrove
Elaine Evans
David Morrison
Chris Parker
Kathy Knight
Ryan Billig
visit their website at  internationalnoveltygamelan.bandcamp.com
As an interesting contrast, for the second half of the album I have added a live recording made in September 2015 in Malang, East Java with the artists of SINGO BUDOYO joined by Arrington de Dionyso on bass clarinet and a short section with throat-singing and bromiophone on part three.
Arrington de Dionyso.credits:
released September 26, 2018
I'd like to dedicate this album to my friend Munir Khar who came to the United States seeking amnesty from political persecution in his home city of Malang, East Java. He was jailed for many months while waiting for his hearings and his story of finally being able to settle in Minneapolis as an artist is a truly extraordinary tale...as are all tales of people who seek refuge and safety from oppression anywhere in the world. I joked with him that maybe someday I will be forced to seek amnesty in Malang. So it's in that spirit that this album features Javanese-inspired trance music meets free jazz improvisations recorded with residents of both Minneapolis and Malang. Does that make sense?

Arrington de Dionyso.artist: HMMM with Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Recorded in Semarang, Java, Indonesia in November 2015
HMMM is a fantastic avant-music trio from Semarang. They are influenced equally by traditional Javanese trance music, No Wave, and Gospel. The guitarist Fredian Bintar reports being a huge Derek Bailey fan, and in some of singer Jane's vocal work I wonder if there isn't a bit of a Yoko Ono influence as well, but the music they make together with Riskia on drums is all their own and unlike anything else I have ever heard happening in contemporary Indonesian music.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Osing Trance Banyuwangi (UNHEARD INDONESIA Volume 8)
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Banyuwangi Putra with Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
OSING TRANCE, recorded in Temenggungan Village, Banyuwangi, East Java Indonesia in December 2015. "Musik Patrol" is a style of bamboo gamelan ensemble. As you can hear, the rhythms are played at a breakneck speed with fascinating shifts in phasing.
The region around Banyuwangi is particularly fascinating from a cultural perspective. As Islam spread across Java, Banyuwangi was the last major enclave of Hinduism before the island of Bali, which is only a few miles across the sea. Even though the region became "officially" Muslim there has always been a continuation of certain practices and beliefs from the pre-Islamic days, and that continuity remains dramatically in the musical styles still performed there. The rapid rhythms and phasing are definitely more in line with what you might expect from a Balinese gamelan, but there is still a kind of Javanese "roughness" in the attack and accents. The overall feel is quite different. The people of this region are Osing, a different ethnic identity than either Javanese or Balinese, with their own language and cultural traditions.
The group "Banyuwangi Putra" is considered a regional favorite. I'm told they have won many contests in Java and are often in demand to perform at many different kinds of cultural festivals and other events.
OSING TRANCE is one of my favorites in the "UNHEARD INDONESIA" series documenting my collaborations with Indonesian musicians during my travels. If you download the full album you will also get to hear a few recordings of a children's gamelan that performed earlier the same day. I would like any Indonesian listener to be able to download the album for free. If you're living outside Indonesia and make more than $500 in one month please consider making a donation for the download as I've invested a considerable amount of my own money to finance both my travels and the editing of these recordings.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Piano Trance 2: Holy Ghost Tones: Benefit for TAS Haitian Art School
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Part two of the "Piano Trance" series is dedicated to the benefit of Timouns Art School in Port-au-Prince, Haiti. ALL funds raised from downloads of this music will go directly to the art school which struggles to exist under extremely difficult and violent circumstances due to the political instability and gang warfare which makes normal life impossible in Haiti. The poorest country in the Western Hemisphere was once the richest land in the entire world, exploited endlessly since the very beginning of the colonial era and the slave trade that ensued.
The recording was made on May 23, 2023 after consuming a handful of Psilocybe ovoideocystidiata that I had harvested a year after inoculating the mycelium underneath a hundred year old black walnut tree. I felt the medicine start to kick in gently at first as I walked along the forest path from the Organic Farm to the Communications Building at the Evergreen State College, where I like to go to use the piano rehearsal rooms as often as I can. I saw the breathtaking beauty of the golden sun shine its beams through the translucent green tree leaves, reminding me of an experience I had almost thirty years ago in this same forest that taught me how to completely reconsider the ways I had been listening to and working with sound. I sang along with the birds and the gentle breeze as I walked, receiving heart affirmations from the ground I walked upon.
Entering the private piano room and closing the door with the lights down low, this album dictated itself to me in its entirety as I played without pause for the 43 minute duration of this recording. "Piano Kebyar" is dedicated to the fierce Barong spirits of Java and Bali in gratitude for both the protection and abundant mischief they provide to their communities. "Slit the Throat of the Slavemaster" is dedicated to the heartbreaking struggle of the Haitian people to gain enduring freedom from the shackles of slavery, colonialism, and the ongoing exploitation both from other nations and from those who spread evil within their own society. May Haiti be FREE!
The album as a whole is especially dedicated to my dear brother Love Leonce, who runs the Timouns Art School in the shantytowns of Port-au-Prince. He works with the belief that providing young people with the transformative experience of art-making will help deter them from the temptation to join the violent gangs that cause only robbery and death in their own communities.
If you wish you can also donate directly through his GoFundMe as well.
gofund.me/cac97be4
We also have a limited number of original paintings for sale created by Love Leonce and his students at TAS. We will be showing some of the paintings and also have prints available for selected works at CaTMA Gallery in Olympia in August 2023. Please message me directly with any questions.
AYIBOBO

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Piano Trance series vol. 1: Saturn in Pisces
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
This album is an act of Magick!
Recorded, mixed, mastered and released within one day to mark the auspicious entry of Saturn into Pisces, to ward off evil influence and exorcise demons!
This my first EVER album release showcasing my work with that most Western Canon of instruments, the illustrious Piano Forte. Let the powerful dream magic of Pisces dissolve whatever stale structures need to be broken down and digested, let the wisdom of Saturn give structure and lay foundation for our wildest spell-work!
What do you think? I felt absolutely possessed while making this and it's been an enormous joy listening back to it. Is it compelling or interesting to anyone else out there to hear what I'm doing on the piano? I would be open to doing more of this in a "proper" studio and maybe a piano-only concert now and then if there is interest. Totally cool if it's not your thing though.
A little background on this record for those who wish to read further: very few people know this, but piano was actually the first instrument I ever played. I would sneak up to the piano after church and pound the keys until a responsible adult would remove me from the holy milieu. Maybe they only heard a child making noise, but I could hear rich symphonies of dancing dragons, rain storms, circus performers and talking trees. For a short time I was "forced" to take lessons which I hated, because it was mind numbingly boring and only about learning the names of notes on the paper, not about unleashing torrents of ecstatic, all-consuming sheets of sound with the massive power of 88 tuned drums.
As I think more upon this theme of "Saturn in Pisces" I remember how I set out early in my musical career to offer the world what I envisioned as a complete and total rejection of all the norms and traditions of "Western Civilization" as I saw it- and how musically speaking at least, a piano in some ways represents the epitome of everything I sought to tear down. The spacing and division of the notes the way they are on a piano isn't "natural" in terms of creating purely intuitive music, as there is a structure determined by the instrument itself that is impossible to completely break away from without significantly altering the instrument itself.
Of course at the same time maybe that is all total nonsense: I've had countless life-changing experiences listening to and even playing with some of the greatest piano players alive, who seem to effortlessly imbue this instrument of "Saturnine structure" with life-affirming creativity and imagination (Pisces). The LIMITATIONS (Saturn) of the instrument as it is handed down to us by Western tradition and world-view determining how sounds should be sounded, played by some hands at least, have the potential of delivering the listener into higher realms of boundless wonder and emotional catharsis. Total freedom? idk
SO what then is the piano repertoire of Arrington de Dionyso? Here I attempt to work within the limitations of the instrument (no special avant-garde "preparations") as well as my own limitations in ability to play it. I touch the piano as a life-force conductor and conjure up some Hoodoo that hearkens a little bit to Stravinsky and Don Van Vliet, along with all those ancient planets of blues, jazz, classical, post-classical and the far-flung moons of post free 60's minimalist potentialities...I also bring in the rich years of experience that delivered me to the center of the horse-trance dances of East Java, the blaring pipes and pounding drums of Jajouka, down the raspy throats of the fire-breathing shamans of Tuva...like those famous mycelial threads on the forest floor, structures are built by connecting points between fragile cells, sometimes falling apart, sometimes changing directions, sometimes making a mess and picking up the blood-stained pieces out of the mud.
Man, I hope you dig it!

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Raw Honey and a Shot of Poison: The Honey and Poison Sessions
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso's Malaikat dan Singa
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
For me, HONEY AND POISON represents the peak of my mid-2010s songwriting craft with the ensemble known as Malaikat dan Singa (Angels and Lions). The album was recorded on a Tascam 388 eight track at the Dub Narcotic Studio in Olympia, Washington throughout 2014 until 2017 and was finally mixed, mastered and released through Bandcamp in 2018. We very recently made the discovery of the original in-board "rough" mixes that were made before the songs were mixed digitally. There are no out-board effects of any kind used in these mixes, all EQ was achieved directly in the Tascam 388 which imparts a very unique sound quality that I like to call the RAW HONEY MIX. There is a very different spatial feeling communicated in these mixes, yes, it's raw but also extremely focused with no filler audio "trickery".
I know a lot of you have already paid good money to get a download of the "Honey and Poison" album. You should feel completely empowered to help yourself to a free download of this version so that you can compare them back to back, and hopefully let me know which you prefer. We're hoping to FINALLY get this issued as a vinyl LP sometime in 2021 or early 2022, and we have the opportunity to choose from which version of the album we would prefer to master the laquers. So...let me know what you think, ok?
As an extra bonus, I've added a couple instrumental tunes from the same sessions that were not available before, and when you download the full album you also get a bonus live album recorded at the Hobbit House sometime in 2018 in Olympia, Washington.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Shouting Over Deep Water Blues
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Recorded by Henrik Lofgren in a boat in the ocean near a small island in Sweden on July 23 2012.
Arrington de Dionyso performs on various Bromiophones and voice using modulations and resonances from the sonic idiosyncrasies of the small rowboat on the waves and various metallic objects used for echoing effects.
"A highly psychedelically charged sonic depth charge focused on Bromiophone and multiphonic voice, recorded with Henrik Lofgren in July of 2012 on an actual boat off the coast of Sweden (as seen in the photos!).
There is a weirdly ASMR vibe to some of these tracks, and a blues meets icaro tinge to others. I encourage you all to listen/download for free/pay what you want."

Arrington de Dionyso.title: SOLAR FLARES
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
SOLAR FLARES is an alchemical journey from the mud and fire of the throat amplified by a tsunami of piano strings and evaporating into the spirit wind of the sacred flute. A map of katabatik descent and ascension saturated with tape hiss immediacy to exorcise all digital demons and banish AI parasitism forever.
SOLAR FLARES was recorded November 3rd, 2025 at Addendum Records studios. All tracks are LIVE with no overdubs- the multiple perspectives in the sounds come from the use of 15 different microphones placed throughout the large studio and individual EQ and compression strategies for each track. The title track also employs live tape echo and saturation from a modified Marantz cassette recorder feeding a bass amp placed underneath a grand piano.
Track one- Throatsinging, stomping, voice mask and tape recorder
Track two- Grand Piano
Track three- Lalove (sacred flute of Central Sulawesi)
For proper listening experience please eat as many mushrooms as you can, two at a time drenched in fresh local honey. Chew without swallowing for at least fifteen minutes or until completely dissolved under the tongue. Play the recording as loudly as you can without upsetting any animals.
Arrington de Dionyso.credits:
released December 11, 2025
Recorded by Mason Bornyasz, mixed and mastered by Arrington de Dionyso.
Cover photo by Oro Whitley!

Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Recorded during the Spring Equinox 2012, using voice, bass clarinet, modified Marantz tape recorder and echoplex, at Dub Narcotic OLYMPIA.
This music is inspired in part by an extraordinary experience I had on a recent tour in Sweden. I performed far away from any city in the ruins of an ancient fortress, feeling the call of the spirits through the rocks and burning branches. Video footage from that performance can be seen HERE=  www.youtube.com/watch?v=LVpMB2Ezjts

trackinfo.title: O Thunder, Perfect Mind
trackinfo.lyrics: I was sent forth from the power, and I have come to those who reflect upon me, and I have been found among those who seek after me. Look upon me, you who reflect upon me, and you hearers, hear me. You who are waiting for me, take me to yourselves. And do not banish me from your sight. And do not make your voice hate me, nor your hearing. Do not be ignorant of me anywhere or any time. Be on your guard! Do not be ignorant of me. For I am the first and the last. I am the honored one and the scorned one. I am the whore and the holy one. I am the wife and the virgin. I am <the mother> and the daughter. I am the members of my mother. I am the barren one and many are her sons. I am she whose wedding is great, and I have not taken a husband. I am the midwife and she who does not bear. I am the solace of my labor pains. I am the bride and the bridegroom, and it is my husband who begot me. I am the mother of my father and the sister of my husband and he is my offspring. I am the slave of him who prepared me. I am the ruler of my offspring. But he is the one who begot me before the time on a birthday. And he is my offspring in (due) time, and my power is from him. I am the staff of his power in his youth, and he is the rod of my old age. And whatever he wills happens to me. I am the silence that is incomprehensible and the idea whose remembrance is frequent. I am the voice whose sound is manifold and the word whose appearance is multiple. I am the utterance of my name. Why, you who hate me, do you love me, and hate those who love me? You who deny me, confess me, and you who confess me, deny me. You who tell the truth about me, lie about me, and you who have lied about me, tell the truth about me. You who know me, be ignorant of me, and those who have not known me, let them know me. For I am knowledge and ignorance. I am shame and boldness. I am shameless; I am ashamed. I am strength and I am fear. I am war and peace. Give heed to me. I am the one who is disgraced and the great one. Give heed to my poverty and my wealth. Do not be arrogant to me when I am cast out upon the earth, and you will find me in those that are to come. And do not look upon me on the dung-heap nor go and leave me cast out, and you will find me in the kingdoms. And do not look upon me when I am cast out among those who are disgraced and in the least places, nor laugh at me. And do not cast me out among those who are slain in violence. But I, I am compassionate and I am cruel. Be on your guard! Do not hate my obedience and do not love my self-control. In my weakness, do not forsake me, and do not be afraid of my power. For why do you despise my fear and curse my pride? But I am she who exists in all fears and strength in trembling. I am she who is weak, and I am well in a pleasant place. I am senseless and I am wise. Why have you hated me in your counsels? For I shall be silent among those who are silent, and I shall appear and speak, Why then have you hated me, you Greeks? Because I am a barbarian among the barbarians? For I am the wisdom of the Greeks and the knowledge of the barbarians. I am the judgement of the Greeks and of the barbarians. I am the one whose image is great in Egypt and the one who has no image among the barbarians. I am the one who has been hated everywhere and who has been loved everywhere. I am the one whom they call Life, and you have called Death. I am the one whom they call Law, and you have called Lawlessness. I am the one whom you have pursued, and I am the one whom you have seized. I am the one whom you have scattered, and you have gathered me together. I am the one before whom you have been ashamed, and you have been shameless to me. I am she who does not keep festival, and I am she whose festivals are many. I, I am godless, and I am the one whose God is great. I am the one whom you have reflected upon, and you have scorned me. I am unlearned, and they learn from me. I am the one that you have despised, and you reflect upon me. I am the one whom you have hidden from, and you appear to me. But whenever you hide yourselves, I myself will appear. For whenever you appear, I myself will hide from you. Those who have [...] to it [...] senselessly [...]. Take me [... understanding] from grief. and take me to yourselves from understanding and grief. And take me to yourselves from places that are ugly and in ruin, and rob from those which are good even though in ugliness. Out of shame, take me to yourselves shamelessly; and out of shamelessness and shame, upbraid my members in yourselves. And come forward to me, you who know me and you who know my members, and establish the great ones among the small first creatures. Come forward to childhood, and do not despise it because it is small and it is little. And do not turn away greatnesses in some parts from the smallnesses, for the smallnesses are known from the greatnesses. Why do you curse me and honor me? You have wounded and you have had mercy. Do not separate me from the first ones whom you have known. And do not cast anyone out nor turn anyone away [...] turn you away and [... know] him not. [...]. What is mine [...]. I know the first ones and those after them know me. But I am the mind of [...] and the rest of [...]. I am the knowledge of my inquiry, and the finding of those who seek after me, and the command of those who ask of me, and the power of the powers in my knowledge of the angels, who have been sent at my word, and of gods in their seasons by my counsel, and of spirits of every man who exists with me, and of women who dwell within me. I am the one who is honored, and who is praised, and who is despised scornfully. I am peace, and war has come because of me. And I am an alien and a citizen. I am the substance and the one who has no substance. Those who are without association with me are ignorant of me, and those who are in my substance are the ones who know me. Those who are close to me have been ignorant of me, and those who are far away from me are the ones who have known me. On the day when I am close to you, you are far away from me, and on the day when I am far away from you, I am close to you. [I am ...] within. [I am ...] of the natures. I am [...] of the creation of the spirits. [...] request of the souls. I am control and the uncontrollable. I am the union and the dissolution. I am the abiding and I am the dissolution. I am the one below, and they come up to me. I am the judgment and the acquittal. I, I am sinless, and the root of sin derives from me. I am lust in (outward) appearance, and interior self-control exists within me. I am the hearing which is attainable to everyone and the speech which cannot be grasped. I am a mute who does not speak, and great is my multitude of words. Hear me in gentleness, and learn of me in roughness. I am she who cries out, and I am cast forth upon the face of the earth. I prepare the bread and my mind within. I am the knowledge of my name. I am the one who cries out, and I listen. I appear and [...] walk in [...] seal of my [...]. I am [...] the defense [...]. I am the one who is called Truth and iniquity [...]. You honor me [...] and you whisper against me. You who are vanquished, judge them (who vanquish you) before they give judgment against you, because the judge and partiality exist in you. If you are condemned by this one, who will acquit you? Or, if you are acquitted by him, who will be able to detain you? For what is inside of you is what is outside of you, and the one who fashions you on the outside is the one who shaped the inside of you. And what you see outside of you, you see inside of you; it is visible and it is your garment. Hear me, you hearers and learn of my words, you who know me. I am the hearing that is attainable to everything; I am the speech that cannot be grasped. I am the name of the sound and the sound of the name. I am the sign of the letter and the designation of the division. And I [...]. (3 lines missing) [...] light [...]. [...] hearers [...] to you [...] the great power. And [...] will not move the name. [...] to the one who created me. And I will speak his name. Look then at his words and all the writings which have been completed. Give heed then, you hearers and you also, the angels and those who have been sent, and you spirits who have arisen from the dead. For I am the one who alone exists, and I have no one who will judge me. For many are the pleasant forms which exist in numerous sins, and incontinencies, and disgraceful passions, and fleeting pleasures, which (men) embrace until they become sober and go up to their resting place. And they will find me there, and they will live, and they will not die again.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Songs of Psychic Fire vol. I
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso and Thollem McDonas
Arrington de Dionyso.about: a series of short, minimalistic duos between bass clarinet and piano, recorded to a 1940's era Wilcox Gay record lathe. This is "post-improvised" music, i.e. these pieces are free improvisations in the sense that players are free to make any choices desired, however the choice in most of these pieces is improvise within a very narrow bandwidth of sound color, emphasizing attack, decay, and the repetition of short interlocking phrases rather than linear melodic or rhythmic development.
Arrington de Dionyso.credits:
released September 11, 2011
Recorded to blue plastic plates using a Wilcox Gay record lathe from the 1940's, on August 9th and 10th 2011 at Le Chateau Circular Ruins in Olympia, Washington.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Suara Naga
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso's Malaikat dan Singa
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Originally released as K Records KLP 226 in 2011.
"Suara Naga" is Indonesian for "The Voice of the Dragon" and this classic album was the second of the "Malaikat dan Singa" albums, which lead to helping me make many connections in Indonesia, with many invitations to organize tours and other performances and collaborations with local musicians.
Arrington de Dionyso's 2011 album Suara Naga [KLP226] is a pleasant balm for any creative spirit.
By "pleasant", I mean incendiary.
By "balm", I mean Cro-Magnon regressive.
By "any", I mean freaks only.
By creative", I mean dangerous.
And by "spirit" I mean the flames of Hell and the flowers of Eden entwined upon the nuptial sofas of pleasure.
By "spirit", I mean wearing grooves in the floor boards of your dirty mind.
By"spirit", I mean that part of you that either heeds the call or gets destroyed.
By"spirit", I mean this force coursing through your corporeal privates.
By "spirit", I mean a mystical draft.
By "spirit", I mean fermented juice.
By "spirit", I mean a sharpened stick rammed through an animal.
By "spirit", I mean you really don't need any more music, this one alone will do.
By "spirit", I mean a catalyst for your art.
By "spirit", I mean, SPIRIT!
Now get out there and play this album at full volume while doing something good.
The Facts:
This is the second Arrington de Dionyso's Malaikat dan Singa album, the first is entitled simply Malaikat dan Singa [KLP215] and was released in 2009.
"Malaikat dan Singa" means "Angels and Lions", "Suara Naga" means "The Dragon's Voice".
All of the lyrics on both records are in Indonesian, which Arrington taught himself specifically for this project.
The album was recorded at Dub Narcotic Studio in Olympia, with an all-star cast including members of LAKE and Angelo Spencer et les Hauts Sommets. The record was produced by Karl "The Genius" Blau.
You know how in the 1950s the press and parents and such talked about how rock'n'roll had some evil, sexual, tribal force that was going to possess the youth? Arrington de Dionyso's Malaikat dan Singa is really that. Trance-punk dancehall explosion gonna knock you up! 2011!
Press
"With a name like Arrington de Dionyso, you can only expect something extremely exotic, and the man always knows how to dish it up." – Tiny Mix Tapes
"You could call it chaos, but (Arrington de Dionyso) seems to know exactly what's going on at all times." - The Washington Post

Arrington de Dionyso.title: THE CASINO a book
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
NOTE: The introductory period is OVER! No more free streaming for this one. Anyways, I'm working on a new book.
*
*
*
"The Casino is dim but for the blinking signs..."
What's all this about?
Way back in 1997 I wrote a book. Now in the final months of 2025 I turned into a "book on tape", and anti-podcast if you will.
The Casino is sort of an extended poem that weaves in and out of a labyrinthine attempt to make sense of a devastating breakup while raising a three year old in poverty. In the middle of the story, the narrator goes to Bolivia on a medical mission, somehow the locals believe he is a doctor.
The text is dripping with myth and metaphor, with dream journal entries woven in seamlessly with the threads of the core narrative. Fans of both Old Time Relijun and Malaikat dan Singa will recognize the seeds of lyrical turns of phrase and poetic themes that later became embedded in song lyrics, especially in the earlier albums by Old Time Relijun. The autobiographical elements may give a broader context for those wishing to explore deeper meaning in the lyrics of songwriter Arrington de Dionyso.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: The Dragon Raga (Songs of Psychic Fire 2020)
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
This album is a follow-up to a record I made in 2008 called "I See Beyond the Black Sun". There was a presidential election that year as well. Pluto was just beginning his entry into the sign of Capricorn. That will mean something to some people, to others it will not.
Sometimes we are faced with such a vision of doom that the end of the entire world seems right up in view and we are but like a deer or a raccoon or a possum staring blankly at two ever brightening disks of light rapidly increasing in size.
IF Doom is approaching, it's been working its way towards us for at least five thousand years. This isn't to say that I am not alarmed or in any way free of the sense of dread and panic that most sensible people are feeling right now. I am however, willing to accept that we are indeed dangling over a precipice where everything we think we know is about to change. We really don't know exactly what comes next.
Ever since the end of February my mantra has been pretty consistent- "We are never going 'back to normal' because whatever it was we were letting ourselves call 'NORMAL' was the very problem to begin with. So I welcome and embrace a total change, but it has been painful! It is GOING to be painful! We need to do what we can to keep each other alive and resist the monstrous powers that try to force us into despair, cynicism, giving up.
I don't believe that artists or musicians have ever really occupied their rightful place in any society- as keepers of wisdom and continuity, and also as healers, dreamers, visionaries to guide us into being enveloped by and embracing whatever it is that comes next.
I also have no idea if listening to this music that I have created is going to make any difference to you in your life right now in this particular moment. I don't know if anyone will look back in ten or twenty or a hundred years and consider this or anything else I have ever done as a legitimate or appropriate response to the turmoils of the early 21st Century. That part isn't up to me. I do believe trying to present you with some kind of "ecstatic rock and roll" explosive type music, designed for performance in compact spaces full of sweaty dancing people, feels a little out of touch in this particular moment. I haven't really been drawn to listen to anything in that category for a while.
Years ago I had a conversation with an older gentleman that I met after a concert in a small basement gallery space in Paris, France that left a big impression on me. It was a wild show of the kind of free improvisational gymnastics I'm sometimes notorious for. He was complimenting the performance, but he also said something that struck me deeply- "It seems to me you wish to use your music to talk to spirits, but somehow you hold back a little because you're also giving a 'performance'- I think you should stop worrying about 'performing' altogether, and just talk to the spirits."
That was more than ten years ago, and I think about those words nearly every time I make music. I strive to talk to spirits with my breath, and hopefully allow them to speak back through the sounds I channel. I know when I miss the mark or lose focus, but if I'm giving space for the improvisation to be authentically guided from a place of BREATH I always know when I'm at least headed in the right direction.
With this album, and really with any music or art that you truly enjoy and appreciate, please consider sharing in some way. I have left a couple tracks open to downloaded for "free" and a few more that you can only listen to if you pay for the download. If you're absolutely broke please let me know and I'll give you a free download, maybe I can even help you out with a little cash. Making and sharing a post about some new music you're enjoying on your social media channels is a PRICELESS GIFT to an independent artist, it goes much much further than you might imagine.

Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso, Michael Moore and McCloud Zicmuse
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
A LOST ALBUM! We don't know if this session is 2002, 2003, or 2004, but it's somewhere within that range. We don't remember exactly where the recording took place but it was either at Michael Moore's or Bert Wilson's house...and for about the first thirty minutes of listening back to this I couldn't for the life of me figure out who the other clarinet player was- but now we know beyond a doubt it's McCloud Zicmuse!
Michael Moore- piano and toy piano
Arrington de Dionyso- bass clarinet, contra-alto clarinet
McCloud Zicmuse- alto clarinet, recorder, and just a tiny bit of singing at the end of track 12!
A note from Tom Dyer of Green Monkey Records.
I first encountered Michael Moore while putting a jazz combo together to play on “Jazz In Olympia” on my “Olympia: A True Story” album last year. I wanted to tell the story of the Oly jazz scene and put together a band made of former members of Obrador and Bert Wilson’s band, two of the most important groups in the Oly Jazz history. Michael was a founding member of Obrador; their piano player. Unfortunately, Michael’s health was too poor to play on the album, so we had no piano player.
In the ensuing year I have gotten to know Michael, who started feeding me unreleased Obrador recordings for my FreeForm Northwest radio show on KAOS 89.3 FM Olympia, including a great recording of them live at McNeil Island federal prison in 1977. I realized there is a wealth of great pre-internet Olympia jazz that is becoming rapidly forgotten as these great players age and I decided to give some of it a public life out in the modern streaming internet world, on a little download/streaming label, OlyJazz. The first release was a previously unreleased Obrador studio album San Francisco, CA 1983.
Recently, Michael gave me a CD from an improv session in the early 2000’s with Arrington de Dionyso and Mr. X. Delightful. And of course, it was the only known copy. I immediately put it on the air. Of course.
I got the music to Arrington - it was his session - and said what do you think? Now we are making this music available to the world for the first time, through Arrington’s bandcamp page and through all major streaming services. This is ephemeral music that existed once in the atmosphere and was captured and now, finally, makes its way to you. Enjoy this little sliver of the world!

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Tiger Poems: This Music is Fucking Intense
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: TIGER POEMS
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
TIGER POEMS: THIS MUSIC IS FUCKING INTENSE.
This is a warning, a promise, and a statement of fact. Prepare for exorcism and a baptism by fire. THIS MUSIC IS FUCKING INTENSE is a raw document of a singular, volcanic session recorded on Thanksgiving Day 2024, channeling primal forces through three master conduits of avant-garde energy.
Arrington de Dionyso (of Old Time Relijun and Malaikat dan Singa) conjures guttural prophecy and reedsplit shrieks through his Voice Mask, while his bass clarinet weaves subterranean melodies from Saturn's core.
Gust Burns attacks the grand piano as a resonant body of wire, wood, and tension, extracting cascades of harmonic fury and percussive dread.
Noel Kennon provides tectonic plate shift mapping the ceremonial space, activating gongs and sculptures that bend time with crushing impact.
This is acoustic psychosis made manifest, capturing ritual where the air itself catches fire. It's the blissful, terrifying moment where structures dissolve into pure feeling.
We offer this album as a FREE (pay what you want) download in celebration of Arrington's 51st birthday. There is an option to order a physical CDR and poster set with a HANDPAINTED cover made to order.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Trust the Drum
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
“TRUST THE DRUM full album now available. This album presents my perspective on the idea of "Shamanic Drumming" as a worthy listening experience. This music is going to be more energizing than relaxing. There are hypnotic qualities that will allow you to go as deep into a trance state as you are willing to go while listening. I recommend listening through good speakers if possible rather than headphones, the reason being is that headphones have a way of "hacking" your neurological response to sound waves by only running those waves through your ear drum. Try to listen with the whole body, particularly paying attention to how the low frequencies affect your breathing, digestion, heart rate and circulation.
The drum used is a 22 inch frame drum custom built for me by the Tuvan master Ayas Irgit. I highly recommend his drums and would be happy to put you in touch if you would like to order an instrument of your own.”

Arrington de Dionyso.title: TSUNAMI BENEFIT Unheard Indonesia vol. 14: Serang Dakko and Friends in Makassar
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Serang Dakko, Hamrin Samad, and Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
SPECIAL NOTE: The musicians involved would like to dedicate this release to the healing and recovery of all those affected by the earthquake and tsunami in Palu, Central Sulawesi. ALL funds raised from this recording will go directly towards the benefit of recovery efforts. These recordings were made in August of 2018 in Makassar, Sulawesi INDONESIA (note of clarification: a different region of Sulawesi, not affected directly by the recent tsunami) with Daeng Serang Dakko, Daeng Hamrin Samad, and their students playing both traditional percussion and pui pui (traditional reed) along with some "experimental" wind instruments built by Hamrin. Arrington de Dionyso plays bass clarinet, bromiophone, and lalove (traditional Kaili flute from Central Sulawesi).
The last track is a free open improvisation ensemble featuring: Hamrin Samad, Basri B. Sila, Khaeruddin, Ancu Batara, Ochang, Serang Dakko, Bram, Arrington de Dionyso and Siswa SMK somba opu.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Tuvan Shamanism Anxiety Killer
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Chingiz Kam
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
Chingiz Kam is the hereditary Brown Bear Shaman of the Tuva Republic. This recording was made during the Spring Equinox of 2025 in Olympia, Washington. We made several attempts to make this recording using digital equipment, but the spirits were absolutely not going to allow it. Instead they were much happier when we brought out the Marantz cassette recorder to make a tape recording of Chingiz's ceremony. This recording is very useful in removing anxiety, it's recommended to turn it up as loudly as you can bear it.
In Chingiz’s own words – “I was born on the banks of the mighty Siberian river Yenisei and raised in the city of Kyzyl, Republic of Tuva. I have the title of Brown Bear Shaman. I come from shamanic ancestry, both on my father’s side and on my mother’s side. Ancestors come from Tes-Khemsky and Ovyursky districts, the Republic of Tuva, which is located on the border with Mongolia.
Childhood and youth were mostly spent at the shepherd’s camp of my grandparents. They have kept their culture of nomadic life up to the present. Every vacation I came to them and helped them with the housework. During this time, my special connection and love for nature and animals was formed. Just like all Tuvans always, I pastured sheep and goats, sang songs in the steppes and in the mountains. I recognized life around me through the prism of Tuvan traditions. During this period, I met many spirits of the mountains, taiga, and rivers of my native places. They sometimes appeared in the form of deer, bear, wolf, snowy owl, eagle and other animals, and sometimes as strong strange short winds.
All my life I clearly felt that someone was watching me closely. They were invisible defenders. When I was very ill, my ancestors came in dreams in the guise of various familiar people and fumigated with the help of artysh (juniper), after which I quickly began to recover. I have never been able to get lost in a dense forest or in an unfamiliar area. I felt many strange inexplicable things very accurately and my correct decisions were formed from this. Some people felt that I was a shaman and asked me to make charms and amulets.
Ancestral shamans also gave life lessons and the necessary knowledge for shamanic practice to one degree or another. They began to actively “work” at the time when I was about 27 years old. At that time, I had already graduated from the university and worked in my specialty. Any technology stopped working next to me, because of this there were problems at work. Reality seemed to become unreal and I experienced health problems, but the results of all tests showed that I was healthy. This went on for a couple of years, until the ancestors of the shamans “in their own ways” found me a mentor – a practicing Tuvan shaman, who is also a member of the “Adyg Eeren” society. I was initiated by three strongest shamans of the Adyg Eeren society, one of whom was the Supreme Shaman of Tuva. So I started my practice in a shamanic clinic in Kyzyl."

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Unheard Indonesia Archival Kaset Series #1 JATHILAN Ojo Sembrono
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Kudho Praneso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
In 2011 I began the "Unheard Indonesia" series to document the different types of collaborations I engage in with the incredible musicians I meet and work with throughout Indonesia. Over the years, I've often received requests to share some of the rare cassette treasures I find on my travels. Dare I say, this is the one cassette that literally started it all. Before ever going to Indonesia for the first time, I was looking for the incredible trance-inducing sounds of "Kuda Lumping", also known as "Jathilan" or "Jaranan" and a host of other names. I initially found this recording on Madrotter's blog and so I knew what I would be setting out to look for upon my first arrival to Java. I bought dozens of Jathilan cassette tapes at Popeye Music in West Yogyakarta, and to my surprise, one of the first tapes I played upon returning home was the very same tape I had first heard on the Madrotter blog.
This is a "campursari" or "mixed" style of Jathilan- you'll notice the propulsive backbeat provided by a Western style "rock" drum kit, and some of the lyrics are taken from popular radio hits of the time. That doesn't diminish one iota (I feel it rather enhances over all) the power of the trance-inducing and ultimately sacred nature of the music itself.
I hooked up a cassette player to a CDR burner and later did my own EQ and mix & master of the music you hear. In fact, this might be one of the most "hi-fi" versions of this cassette that has ever been heard!
This music is NOT for profit of any kind and I make no claims of ownership. I only share these cassettes because this music is so important to me, and it is basically next to impossible to find this cassette today, so even many music lovers within Indonesia are unable to enjoy this music unless it is shared through an accessible online platform such as bandcamp.

from Halo Malang website:
Kolaborasi apik tersaji kala tiga basis kesenian berada dalam satu ruang bernama 'Dokar Berbunyi', Rabu (30/08/2017) di Kec. Dau, Kab. Malang.
Tarian Jaran Kepang atau Reog Ponorogo adalah hal yang sudah lazim kita lihat, dibawa ke level berbeda dengan iringan Trance Music Arrington De Dionyso dan gerak dinamis video mapping Etnicholic Project. Sebuah rumah di tengah permukiman Dusun Banjartengah dijadikan media proyeksi. Nuansa surealis semakin hidup kala aroma kemenyan berbaur di antara ratusan warga yang melingkari arena utama pertunjukan. Suara cambuk memecah dinginnya malam, para penari mulai kesurupan.
"Bisa jadi, ini kolaborasi pertama di Malang ketika Jaranan digabung dengan teknologi mapping video. Kita juga kehadiran tamu dari Washington USA, mas Arrington," ujar Redy Eko Prastyo seniman asal Malang pegiat di Etnicholic Project.
Kehadiran Arrington dan Etnicholic kali ini dalam giat pra-event Festival Kampung Dokar #2 yang akan digelar pada bulan Oktober 2017. Arrington adalah musisi eksperimental pendiri Olympia Experimental Music Festival yang sangat tertarik dengan musik-musik tradisional yang berhubungan dengan 'kerasukan/kesurupan'. Musik dalam seni jaranan adalah salah satunya. Malaikat dan Singa adalah grup musik baru yang Arrington bentuk. Sedangkan Redy, adalah musisi etnik yang juga pegiat di Jaringan Kampung Nusantara. Kolaborasi keduanya memang tak asing. Sebelumnya mereka pernah satu panggung di Festival Kampung Cempluk 2013. Arrington kala itu membawa nama Malaikat dan Singa Indonesia Journey#2.
"Masyarakat Dusun Banjartengah harus berbangga dengan event ini. Karena ini tak hanya kolaborasi lintas benua, juga lintas budaya," sambung Redy.
Kesenian Jaranan/Reog lokal yang menjadi gerak utama adalah Turonggo Sekar Remaja, Dau. Satu yang menjadi perhatian selain tiupan Saxophone Arrington adalah gerak lincah Azam (siwa TK 0 Besar) asal Pujon yang menjadi Bujang Ganong sebelum atraksi reog. Seniman cilik ini belajar otodidak sejak usia 3 tahun. Musisi karinding 'Unen-unen' asal Tuban juga meramaikan Dokar Berbunyi.
"Kampung Dokar kini juga dikenal di luar negeri. Dengan Kampung Dokar, ini menjadi bukti kalau persaudaraan tidak dibatasi oleh, desa, kampung atau negara. Semoga ini menjadi semangat baru untuk warga Banjartengah ini," ujar Hasan Asyari, Kepala Desa Sumbersekar.
Meski singkat, Arrington rupanya cukup fasih mengucapkan salam perkenalan dalam bahasa Jawa. "Kulo Arrington, matur nuwun untuk semua warga di sini. Saya senang di Kampung Dokar,"

Arrington de Dionyso.title: UNHEARD INDONESIA vol. 16 : Return to Mojowarno- Jaranan Tung Dor 2015
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Arrington de Dionyso and Jaranan Tung Dor of Mojowarno, East Java
Arrington de Dionyso.about: Volume 16 in the "Unheard Indonesia" series which documents the various types of collaborative settings in which I have been invited to perform while traveling throughout Indonesia. This recording was made in 2015 and is a follow up to Volume 4 of the series, as both editions document recordings made with the same community of Jaranan musicians in Mojowarno, East Java. "Jaranan Tung Dor" is believed (at least according to what I am told by musicians in East Java) to be the oldest style of Jaranan- you'll notice there are no gongs of any kind in this performance- only drums. Normally the only melodic element would be the vocalist- as this is a collaboration between myself and the community you will hear that I provide my services with the bass clarinet.

Arrington de Dionyso.title: Unheard Indonesia vol. 17 REAK Juarta Putra with Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.artist: Juarta Putra with Arrington de Dionyso
Arrington de Dionyso.about:
I'm so excited to be able to share this music with the wider world, one of the most life affirming international collaborations I've been involved with in recent memory.
JUARTA PUTRA is a group performing the traditional REAK music and dance of West Java, Indonesia. They're based in Jatinangor, just east of Bandung. Track one was recorded in Jakarta at Kios Ojo Keos during our rehearsal preparing for the performance that took place at Synchronize Festival at the Jakarta Expo Center on October 4th 2024. The rest were recorded at their Sanggar (community center) in Jatinangor on October 18th, edited from a session about two and a half hours in total duration.
What is REAK? Reak is a very popular style of West Javanese trance music, related to the Jathilan or "Kuda Lumping" horse trance dances found in other regions of Java, but with a very distinct Sundanese flair. You don't usually encounter any gongs in Reak, rather the large bedug bass drum is the central instrument surrounded by a multitude of other drums of varying sizes called dogdog beating out a multivalent hypnotic train of throbbing polyrhythms that will speed up or slow down at crucial moments during the ceremony. The drums are accompanied by the piercing snake charmer's wail of the double-reeded tarompet, almost always driven through a primitive amplifier cone powered by a car battery. In these recordings Juarta Putra is also joined by American avant-garde artist Arrington de Dionyso on the bass clarinet!
Reak ceremonies also include a dancing Barong- kind of like a Lion meets Dragon hybrid creature that guards temples and other holy places, in Reak the Barong is at turns frightfully hideous and hilariously comical, with his dance full of acrobatic antics that might include incredible somersaults and leaps into the air, followed by asking members of the audience to throw some money down his throat.
Reak defies dualistic divisions of what is sacred or profane, it is both an incredibly sacred healing ceremony and ludicrously confrontational performance art that is enjoyed by the entire community.
It is normal for the dancers and sometimes even audience members to go into trance, becoming possessed by the spirits of ancestors or local nature spirits. There are different "levels" of trance possession according to the type of healing one might need, and the experience level of the participants. The Malim is a shaman-like figure who stands guard during the ceremony monitoring the dancers as they become trance-possessed, he is able to determine the types of spirits that manifest themselves during the ceremony and acts to make sure that the ritual maintains a safe container for the spirits to dance without causing harm.
If you would like to see footage of Reak rituals there are thousands of great videos of Reak Sunda on YouTube. Here is a documentary I produced with a different group, Sanca Birawa, in 2015:  www.youtube.com/watch?app=desktop&v=BGb6LJsCf4A
There is also another recording of Juarta Putra recorded by Luigi Monteanni that is available as an LP from Discrepant Records via bandcamp:  luigimonteanni.bandcamp.com/album/the-sacred-entertainment-r-ak-ceremonial-horse-trance-music-from-priangan
also
Read more at The Attic:
theatticmag.com/reports/2277/investigating-the-sundanese-ritual-of-kasenian-r%C3%A9ak.html
